summary: Recursive n-th prime Fibonacci on u64 with two partial_fixpoint helpers (has_divisor_at trial division, prime_fib_at Fibonacci stepping) whose termination depends on the open conjecture of infinitely many prime Fibonaccis; specs are stated universally but proved only on a finite safe range (n.toNat ≤ 6 / ≤ 5) via case-split helpers `n_eq_of_toNat_le_N` that turn small u64 toNat bounds into disjunctions over concrete u64 constants (UInt64.ofNat_eq_of_toNat_eq + omega), with each `prime_fib n = RustM.ok v` pin closed by `native_decide` evaluating through the partial_fixpoint kernel — Nat-level oracles `is_prime_nat` and `is_fibonacci_nat` decouple postconditions from the impl, primality witnesses use a sqrt-bound reduction `is_prime_nat_of_sqrt_check` (factor-pair min(k,q) argument giving O(√n) trial divisions), and the no-prime-fib-skipped clause relies on a finite-list characterisation of Fibonacci numbers below 1600 (`is_fibonacci_nat_values`) proved by combining a witness bound `fib_witness_bound` (from `fib_monotone`, itself by Nat.le induction on top of `fib_pos` + `fib_le_succ`) with an explicit `fib_values_le_16` enumeration, then discharging each branch via `rcases ... <;> first | (exfalso; omega) | exact not_is_prime_nat_<v>`.
